questions.question: What is a remote in the context of Git?
options: A branch that exists only on your local machine
A copy of your repository hosted on a server
A configuration file that stores user credentials
A staging area for commits before they are finalized
questions.answer: A copy of your repository hosted on a server

questions.question: What is the default name Git gives to the remote you cloned from or first added?
questions.answer: origin

questions.question: Which command uploads your local commits to a remote repository?
questions.answer: git push

questions.question: What does 'git pull' do?
options: Uploads your commits to the remote
Downloads new commits from a remote without merging
Downloads and merges new commits from a remote
questions.answer: Downloads and merges new commits from a remote

questions.question: What does 'git fetch' do differently from 'git pull'?
options: It downloads new commits WITHOUT merging them
It uploads commits to the remote server
It downloads and merges commits automatically
questions.answer: It downloads new commits WITHOUT merging them

questions.question: What does 'git remote -v' display?
options: A verbose list of remotes with their fetch and push URLs
The commit history in verbose mode
A list of all local branches
questions.answer: A verbose list of remotes with their fetch and push URLs

questions.question: When running 'git remote -v', how many times does each remote appear and why?
options: Once, for pushing and pulling combined
Twice, once for fetch and once for push
Three times, once for fetch, push, and merge
It depends on how many branches track that remote
questions.answer: Twice, once for fetch and once for push

questions.question: What does the '-u' flag do in 'git push -u origin main'?
options: Forces the push to overwrite remote changes
Uploads all branches to the remote
Sets origin/main as the upstream tracking branch for local main
questions.answer: Sets origin/main as the upstream tracking branch for local main

questions.question: After running 'git push -u origin main' once, what command can you use for subsequent pushes?
options: git push -u origin main (the -u flag is always required)
questions.answer: git push

questions.question: Which command adds a new remote named 'origin' to your local repository?
questions.answer: git remote add origin <url>

questions.question: What does 'git remote show origin' display?
options: Only the URL of the remote
Only the list of remote branches
Detailed information including URLs, tracked branches, and push/pull configuration
The commit log of the remote repository
questions.answer: Detailed information including URLs, tracked branches, and push/pull configuration

questions.question: Which platforms are mentioned as examples of servers that can host remote repositories?
questions.answer: GitHub, GitLab, and Bitbucket

questions.question: What command removes a remote named 'backup'?
questions.answer: git remote remove backup

questions.question: What command renames a remote from 'origin' to 'github'?
questions.answer: git remote rename origin github

questions.question: Why might you have multiple remotes configured for one repository?
options: Git requires at least two remotes to function properly
It is common in open-source to have your fork and the original repo as separate remotes
Multiple remotes are needed to push to different branches
You need one remote per collaborator on the project
questions.answer: It is common in open-source to have your fork and the original repo as separate remotes

questions.question: What do remote-tracking branches like 'origin/main' represent?
options: A local branch that is automatically synced in real time
The state of branches on the remote the last time you communicated with it
A backup copy of your local main branch
A branch that can only be modified on the remote server
questions.answer: The state of branches on the remote the last time you communicated with it

questions.question: Which command shows all branches, including remote-tracking branches?
questions.answer: git branch -a

questions.question: What prefix do remote-tracking branches have when listed with 'git branch -a'?
questions.answer: remotes/origin/

questions.question: When creating a new repository on GitHub to connect with an existing local repo, should you initialize it with a README?
options: Yes, always initialize with a README
No, because you already have a local repo with history
Yes, but only if you have no local commits
It does not matter either way
questions.answer: No, because you already have a local repo with history

questions.question: After running 'git fetch origin', what happens to your working directory?
options: It is updated with the latest remote changes
Nothing changes; fetched commits update remote-tracking branches only
New files from the remote appear as untracked files
questions.answer: Nothing changes; fetched commits update remote-tracking branches only

questions.question: Which command lets you see where 'origin/main' is relative to your local 'main' after fetching?
questions.answer: git log --oneline --all --graph

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of remotes in Git?
options: To create backups of configuration files
To enable collaboration by allowing multiple people to push and pull changes
questions.answer: To enable collaboration by allowing multiple people to push and pull changes

questions.question: What is the correct sequence to make a change and push it to an already-configured remote?
options: git push, git commit, git add
git add, git push, git commit
git add, git commit, git push
git commit, git add, git push
questions.answer: git add, git commit, git push

questions.question: Which command is used to manage remote connections in Git?
questions.answer: git remote

questions.question: What happens if you run 'git push' without first setting an upstream branch with '-u'?
options: Git automatically creates a remote repository for you
Git pushes to all configured remotes
Git may display an error asking you to set the upstream branch
questions.answer: Git may display an error asking you to set the upstream branch
